suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note that stable Rust does not provide OnceLock::get_or_try_init, so workers implementing single-flight lazy initialization should expect to use a custom gate or another stable-compatible pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of auto-crypto-single-flight-per-model found a valid implementation and recorded worker feedback that the task naturally suggested get_or_try_init before falling back to a custom AutoCryptoInit gate in core/src/crypto.rs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refine the failing-first testing guidance for compile-boundary work so trybuild-style diagnostics can be proven either by introducing the focused misuse fixture before implementation or by explicitly allowing the first meaningful run to fail on stderr mismatch after the diagnostic lands.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of crypto-attribute-secure-field-boundary found the feature correct, but the worker had to deviate from the skill because the new compile-fail fixture only became meaningful once the macro emitted the diagnostic and the first targeted trybuild run failed on expected stderr reconciliation.
